meta.title: Julio Morcillo · Multidisciplinary Designer in Mérida — Branding, Web & Digital Content
meta.description: Julio Morcillo, multidisciplinary designer in Mérida. I design brands and digital experiences that work as systems: identity, web and content connected by the same visual logic.
meta.keywords: multidisciplinary design, branding, visual identity, web design, digital content, Mérida, Yucatán, Mexico, portfolio, freelance designer Mexico
meta.ogDescription: Branding, web, and digital content for brands that need to work as one clear system.
meta.ogImageAlt: Julio Morcillo portfolio — multidisciplinary designer based in Mérida
drawer.label: Mobile menu
hero.lede: I design brands and digital experiences that work as systems: identity, web and content connected by the same visual logic.
clients.label: Clients
perfil.title: What I do, and how.
perfil.manifesto.label: Profile
perfil.p1: I connect visual identity, websites and digital content so a brand can look and work as a system. I want to understand what a project needs to solve before deciding how it should look.
perfil.p2: I have worked with brands across B2B technology, healthcare, agribusiness, food, telecommunications and professional services. In some projects I led the visual system; in others I translated an existing art direction into concrete applications.
works.title: Selected work.
work.consentido.title: ConSentido
work.consentido.desc: I collaborated on rolling a winery brand out into real applications: brandbook, sub-brands, stationery, activation materials and digital assets. The challenge was holding visual coherence without flattening the differences between locations.
work.consentido.meta.client.label: Client
work.consentido.meta.type.label: Role
work.consentido.meta.scope.label: Authorship
work.consentido.meta.deliverables.label: Deliverables
work.amxitech.title: AMXiTech
work.amxitech.desc: Web design for AMXiTech, a technology sub-brand of Amerimex. I translated the new visual direction into a clear, responsive, commercially oriented experience — built to explain complex IT services and support B2B lead generation.
work.amxitech.meta.client.label: Client
work.amxitech.meta.role.label: Role
work.amxitech.meta.platform.label: Platform
work.amxitech.meta.site.label: Site
work.visummit.title: VI Summit
work.visummit.desc: I collaborated on producing and adapting the event identity across signage, merchandising, digital assets and editorial materials — keeping visual consistency and technical clarity across formats.
work.pollos.title: Los Pollos Giros
work.pollos.desc: Visual identity for a fast-food concept with a distinct personality. The system is built around a simple, memorable character designed to work across packaging, shirts, bags and signage.
work.neuromuscular.title: Neuromuscular MID
work.neuromuscular.desc: Live site for a physiotherapy clinic in Mérida. I designed a clear, warm experience focused on appointment booking, clinical trust and simple navigation for patients.
work.alfa.title: Alfa Comunicaciones
work.alfa.desc: Visual identity for a telecommunications company that needed to stand out without relying on industry clichés. I developed the symbol, palette, typography, usage rules and core applications.
work.consentido.alt: Brandbook and brand applications mockup for ConSentido — a winery system across Uruguay and Mexico
work.amxitech.alt: AMXiTech website — B2B landing page for Amerimex IT services

approach.title: How I work.
approach.01.title: Understand first
approach.01.body: Before opening any tool, I need to understand what problem the brand solves, for whom and in what context. Without that, any visual direction is guesswork.
approach.02.title: System, not pieces
approach.02.body: A logo alone is not enough. Each piece needs to hold the same visual logic — from a business card to a campaign or a website.
approach.03.title: Execute with judgment
approach.03.body: I move between branding, web and content without losing coherence. Sometimes I lead the visual system; other times I translate an existing direction into real pieces.
quote.text:
From logo to campaign,
each piece supports the same system.
xp.title: Where I've worked.
xp.intuitiva.desc: Main formal experience, from late 2023 to December 2025. I worked across branding, web design, digital content, final art and visual applications for clients in technology, healthcare, agribusiness and B2B services.
xp.frzt.desc: Independent practice where I developed projects for small businesses — visual identity, web design and content. Los Pollos Giros belongs to this stage.
xp.pemol.desc: First professional design job. Sports uniform design for sublimation and screen printing, technical files and graphic production applied to apparel.
